verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein expressed in the epididymis and involved in sperm-egg recognition during fertilization. It is secreted into the epididymal lumen and binds to sperm surfaces, likely as a stably associated coat protein or via covalent/wash-resistant anchoring to sperm-surface receptors. The protein reaches the outer face of sperm plasma membrane (and possibly egg plasma membrane during fertilization) through stable association with a membrane partner rather than via its own intrinsic membrane anchor. This qualifies as contextual surface accessibility restricted to the reproductive / gamete context and dependent on stable surface attachment to a TM partner.
